reasoning: The STLFSI4 tracks financial market stress. Normally, it exhibits strong mean reversion (the long-term target mean is 0, but over the last year it has averaged around -0.6 to -0.8). In mid-April 2025, the index spiked sharply to 0.58 but has since rapidly dropped to -0.20 on Apr 18 and -0.26 on Apr 25. This steep downward momentum strongly suggests the acute stress event is resolving. We expect the index to continue its reversion towards its recent low baseline. Therefore, the probability of it reversing and ending up higher than -0.26 by May 4 is low. Over longer horizons, there is more time for a new, unrelated shock to occur, or for the macro backdrop to structurally change, pulling the probability closer to 50% as the forecast horizon extends.
